content.text:
I inspected both pricing nodes. Both exist and are in status = Draft, but neither contains any exposures/locations/TSI data needed for normal pricing calculations.

Current items found:
- @Cornerstone/Tesla/2026
  - id: 2026, underwritingYear: 2026
  - status: Draft
  - No exposures/locations/TSI fields present in content
- @Cornerstone/Nestle/2026
  - id: 2026, underwritingYear: 2026
  - status: Draft
  - No exposures/locations/TSI fields present in content

Before I run the pricing logic I need direction because required data is missing. Please choose one of the options below (or provide exposures/parameters):

Options
1) Proceed using the existing content and treat missing exposures as zero (i.e., AggregatedTSI = 0, TotalLocations = 0). I will run validations, set LastUpdated to now, and (a) either keep status = Draft and record validation alerts about missing exposures, or (b) update status Draft -> Quoted despite missing exposures if you instruct me to. Tell me which status behavior you want.

2) Do not run — please provide the exposures/locations/TSI data to be used in the run (preferred if accurate results required). If you choose this, provide the data (e.g., list of locations with TSI values or a total TSI and number of locations).

3) Run but treat missing exposures as a validation error that prevents status change (i.e., run validations, update AggregatedTSI/TotalLocations = 0, set LastUpdated, leave status = Draft and report validation errors).

Also confirm:
- Do you want me to update the nodes in the mesh with the run results (yes/no)? (Per your instructions, I’ll update content only for these existing nodes if you confirm.)
- If you choose to allow a status change, confirm Draft -> Quoted is acceptable for these runs.

Once you confirm which option and whether to apply status changes, I will run the pricing logic and update the mesh, then return concise run summaries for both pricings.
